findings.title: North Star Metric: User Engagement
findings.description: The North Star metric for SmartNotify is the Daily Active Users (DAU), which reflects the number of unique users engaging with the product daily. This metric is crucial as it indicates overall user engagement and satisfaction with the notification system.
assumptions: User engagement is directly correlated with the effectiveness of notification prioritization.
recommendations: Monitor DAU trends weekly to assess engagement changes.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR)
findings.description: The Notification Click-Through Rate (CTR) measures the percentage of notifications that users interact with. This is a primary KPI as it indicates the effectiveness of the notifications being sent.
assumptions: Higher CTR indicates better notification relevance and user engagement.
recommendations: Aim to increase CTR by optimizing notification content and timing.

findings.title: Primary KPI: Unsubscribe Rate
findings.description: The Unsubscribe Rate measures the percentage of users opting out of notifications. This is a key indicator of user satisfaction and the effectiveness of the notification system.
assumptions: Lower unsubscribe rates indicate higher user satisfaction with notifications.
recommendations: Implement feedback mechanisms to understand reasons for unsubscribing.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: Average Notifications per User
findings.description: The Average Notifications per User metric tracks the number of notifications sent to each user. This is a guardrail metric to ensure that users are not overwhelmed by excessive notifications.
assumptions: Maintaining a reasonable number of notifications is crucial to prevent notification fatigue.
recommendations: Monitor this metric closely to avoid spikes that could lead to user fatigue.

findings.title: Guardrail Metric: 95th Percentile Delivery Latency
findings.description: The 95th Percentile Delivery Latency measures the time it takes for notifications to be delivered to users. This is a guardrail metric to ensure timely delivery of notifications.
assumptions: Lower latency improves user experience and engagement.
recommendations: Reduce latency to under 200ms as per SLO.

findings.title: Instrumentation Gap: User Feedback on Notification Preferences
findings.description: There is a gap in collecting user feedback regarding their preferences for notification types and timings. This data is essential for improving notification relevance and reducing fatigue.
assumptions: User preferences significantly influence engagement and satisfaction.

evidence.excerpt: Implement ML-based notification batching based on user engagement patterns.

recommendations: Develop a user feedback mechanism to gather preferences on notifications.

findings.title: Experiment Opportunity: Notification Timing Optimization
findings.description: Conduct an experiment to test different notification timing strategies based on user engagement patterns. This will help determine the optimal times for sending notifications to maximize CTR.
assumptions: Timing of notifications affects user engagement.

evidence.excerpt: Batch low-priority notifications into a digest delivered at optimal times.

recommendations: Run A/B tests with different timing strategies and measure CTR.

summary: The analysis identifies key metrics for SmartNotify, including a North Star metric of Daily Active Users and primary KPIs like Notification CTR and Unsubscribe Rate. Guardrail metrics ensure user experience is maintained, while gaps in user feedback collection and opportunities for experimentation on notification timing are highlighted.
